title: actinodaphne gullavara
intro: Actinodaphne gullavara is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, native to the Western Ghats in India. It is used in traditional medicine for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds. Herbal preparations of Actinodaphne gullavara include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are often used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
